name: Simple Interest

researchNotes: 4 genuine real past-paper hits across 3 years (2022, 2024, 2025), covering 4 distinct shapes: doubling-time, plain calculation, fractional-rate-with-months, and two-loan comparison. Strong evidence base — only 2 authored items added to round out coverage, both clearly tagged.

reading.intro: Simple Interest is 1 of 10 named Aptitude sub-skills. Real exam evidence shows TNPSC favors word-problem variants (doubling time, comparing two loans, fractional rates with month-based time) over the bare 'calculate SI' formula alone.

qa.question: Formula: What is the Simple Interest formula, and how do you get the doubling time from it?
qa.answer: SI = (Principal × Rate × Time) / 100. A sum DOUBLES when SI equals the Principal itself: P = (P×R×T)/100 → T = 100/R years. So at 8% per annum, doubling time = 100/8 = 12.5 years = 150 months.

qa.question: Real exam (2024): In how many months will Rs.2,000 double itself at 8% per annum simple interest?
Real exam (2022): What is the simple interest on Rs.1,000 for one year at 10% per annum?
Real exam (2024): Find the simple interest on Rs.68,000 at 16⅔% per annum for 9 months.
qa.answer: Convert the mixed-fraction rate: 16⅔% = 50/3 %. Convert months to years: 9 months = 9/12 = 3/4 year. SI = 68,000 × (50/3) × (3/4) / 100 = 68,000 × 12.5/100 = Rs.8,500. (The 3s cancel neatly between the rate's denominator and the time's numerator — recognizing that shortcut avoids messy fraction arithmetic.)

qa.question: Real exam (2025): A borrows Rs.16,000 at 12% p.a. simple interest, B borrows Rs.12,000 at 18% p.a. simple interest. In how many years will their total amounts owed (principal+interest) become equal?
Method — finding the rate when given principal, time, and final amount: A sum of Rs.5,000 becomes Rs.6,200 in 3 years at simple interest. Find the rate.
qa.answer: SI = Amount − Principal = 6200−5000 = 1200. Rearranged formula: R = (SI×100)/(P×T) = (1200×100)/(5000×3) = 120000/15000 = 8% per annum.
